---
title: "nitrix.stats.inference.tfce"
generated: true
signature-hash: cbff6a622e477ad7
description: "Threshold-free cluster enhancement."
listing:
  id: nitrix-stats-inference-tfce-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-stats-inference-tfce/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.inference.tfce`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Threshold-free cluster enhancement.

Threshold-free cluster enhancement (TFCE) replaces an arbitrary cluster-forming
threshold with an integral over all thresholds, weighting each voxel by its
supra-threshold cluster extent:

$$
\operatorname{TFCE}(v)
    = \int_{h=0}^{\operatorname{stat}(v)}
      \operatorname{extent}(v, h)^{E}\, h^{H}\, \mathrm{d}h
$$

where $\operatorname{extent}(v, h)$ is the size of the connected
component containing voxel $v$ when the image is thresholded at height
$h$. The defaults $E = 0.5$, $H = 2.0$ are the 3-D volume
settings; surface and 1-D maps use different exponents and `connectivity`.

The integral is a fixed `n_steps` Riemann sum over $h \in (0, \max]$,
so the step adapts to each map's range (as in FSL) while the *shape* stays
fixed, making the kernel friendly to `jax.vmap` / `jax.lax.scan`
across permutations. Each step is a single
`connected_components` call (jit-able, fixed-shape).
Two-sided enhancement adds the TFCE of the negative part on its disjoint
support, giving a non-negative magnitude map suitable for two-sided
max-statistic family-wise error control.

Built on `connected_components` and
[`cluster_size_map`](nitrix-stats-inference-cluster/cluster_size_map.qmd). It is *not*
differentiable through the discrete cluster forming; like
`connected_components` itself it is an inference
kernel rather than a smooth score.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:__module__ -->
### References

Smith, S. M., & Nichols, T. E. (2009). Threshold-free cluster enhancement:
addressing problems of smoothing, threshold dependence and localisation in
cluster inference. *NeuroImage*, 44(1), 83-98.
https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuroimage.2008.03.061
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-stats-inference-tfce-symbols}
:::
